author: y4rr
content: Done. Bundle delivered: GpuError::Unsupported { op: &'static str, dtype: &'static str } variant added to ferrotorch-gpu/src/error.rs (Display + Error::source updated; #[non_exhaustive] enum so no downstream compile break). 119 silent-fallback sites migrated across kernels.rs (104 sites in 4 clusters) + bf16.rs (15 map_err+eprintln sites). 19 eprintln! removed. 2 std::fs::write('/tmp/*.ptx') debug dumps removed. 6 dead CPU helper functions deleted (cpu_fallback_binary/_unary/_broadcast_binary plus their f64 variants). Workspace audit (literal grep -rn 'match.*GpuError\|GpuError::' --include='*.rs' /home/doll/ferrotorch/ | grep -v 'ferrotorch-gpu/' | grep -v '.claude/'): 0 hits. Only external consumer is ferrotorch-llama/src/gpu.rs:721 map_gpu_err using Display formatting — no exhaustive match, no breakage. Verification: cargo check PASS, cargo clippy -D warnings PASS, cargo fmt PASS, cargo build --workspace --all-targets PASS. cargo test: 260 PASS / 6 FAIL — the 6 failures are post-migration correct behaviour: tests in test_gpu_autograd previously 'passed' silently via CPU fallback; they now surface PtxCompileFailed { kernel: 'reduce_sum_kernel' } because this box has no CUDA GPU. The discipline is working as designed (PyTorch parity: tests that depend on CUDA correctly fail on no-CUDA boxes). User decision needed: mark the 6 tests #[ignore = 'requires CUDA'] as a follow-up, or accept failing tests as a CI gate. Other findings spotted for separate dispatch: hand-rolled Display+Error on GpuError (thiserror migration), unsafe blocks without SAFETY in kernels/bf16, try_launch_* internal Ok(None) pattern cleanup.
